items.title: GLASS cookies
items.summary: Cookies for the GLASS gangmaster's licensing service

items.title: Guidance: Changes gangmasters must report to keep a licence up to date
items.summary: Guidance on the changes that holders of gangmaster's licences must report, to keep their licence up to date.

items.title: The Fair Work Agency licensing standards for gangmasters
items.summary: The Fair Work Agency licensing standards for gangmasters are the conditions labour providers need to meet to get or renew a gangmaster's licence.

items.title: Guidance: Food packaging companies exempt from a gangmaster’s licence
items.summary: Companies that do not need a gangmaster’s licence when providing a food packaging service.

items.title: Accessibility statement for GLASS (gangmaster’s licence service)
items.summary: This accessibility statement applies to the gangmaster’s licence portal and Active Check portal at glass.fairworkagency.gov.uk.

items.summary: How to contact the Fair Work Agency (FWA)

items.title: Guidance: Inspections and licensing decisions for gangmasters
items.summary: How Fair Work Agency officers apply the licensing standards when making decisions about gangmaster licences.

items.title: Form: Complain about pay and work rights
items.summary: Use the online form to complain about not being paid the National Minimum Wage, employment agencies, gangmasters or working hours.

items.title: Guidance: How to apply for or renew a gangmaster's licence
items.summary: How to apply for or renew a licence to provide or use workers in agriculture, horticulture, shellfish gathering, or processing and packaging of food or drink.

items.title: The Fair Work Agency decision policy on gangmasters' licences
items.summary: A detailed explanation of how the Fair Work Agency makes decisions about issuing gangmasters' licences to labour providers.

items.title: Guidance: How to check if a labour provider has a gangmaster’s licence
items.summary: Guidance for businesses that use labour providers to supply workers to the agriculture, horticulture, shellfish, and food processing and packaging sectors.

items.title: Guidance: Fair Work Agency inspections and investigations of labour users
items.summary: Guidance for businesses that use labour providers to supply workers for the agriculture, horticulture, shellfish, and food processing and packaging sectors.

items.title: Guidance: Making a complaint to the Fair Work Agency (FWA) about your recruitment agency
items.summary: This guidance is provided to help agency workers who have raised or need to raise a complaint to FWA about their recruitment agency.

items.title: Notice: Fair Work Agency note: Direct engagement models of introduction in the recruitment sector
items.summary: Fair Work Agency note on the direct engagement model of introduction.

items.title: Guidance: List of people banned from running an employment agency or business
items.summary: Details of people who have been banned from running an employment agency or business, including the period of the ban.

items.title: Guidance: Employment status and employment rights
items.summary: Guidance on employment status for HR advisers, and to help individuals and businesses understand which employment rights apply to them.

items.title: How to report serious abuse or exploitation at work
items.summary: What serious abuse or exploitation at work could mean and how to report it for investigation.

items.title: Tutoring services: guidance for complying with Fair Work Agency (FWA) legislation
items.summary: This guidance aims to help tutoring services to comply with the requirements of the Employment Agencies Act 1973 (the act) and Conduct of Employment Agencies and Employment Businesses Regulations 2003 (the conduct regulations).

items.title: Minimum wage: seafarers and other people working at sea
items.summary: Guidance on when seafarers and other persons who work at sea are entitled to the minimum wage.

items.title: Guidance: Fair Work Agency guidance for providers of interpreting services
items.summary: This guidance aims to help providers of interpreting services comply with the Employment Agencies Act 1973 and Conduct of Employment Agencies and Employment Businesses Regulations 2003.

items.title: Notice: CCUS East Coast Cluster: Teesside selection process
items.summary: Apply to take part in the ECC Teesside selection process for projects seeking connection to the Teesside network by 2032.

items.title: Decision: North Killingholme Power Project: non-material change, Planning Act 2008
items.summary: Decision by the Secretary of State on an application for a non-material change relating to a new thermal generating station that will operate as a Combined Cycle Gas Turbine (CCGT) plant.

items.title: Warm Home Discount (WHD): cost recovery
items.summary: We are seeking views on whether WHD costs should move from standing charges to the per-unit rate.

items.title: Oil and gas: offshore environmental legislation
items.summary: Environmental regulations and guidance on offshore oil and gas exploration and production offshore gas unloading and storage and offshore carbon dioxide storage activities

items.summary: Data to monitor the installation of energy efficiency measures in domestic properties via the Warm Homes: Local Grant scheme in England.

items.summary: Provisional estimates of UK territorial greenhouse gas emissions for 2025, including quarterly emissions estimates.

items.summary: List of special feature articles by subject published in Energy Trends since 2017.

items.summary: Data on the progress against the Clean Power 2030 metrics as published in Energy Trends.

items.summary: Special feature article from the March 2026 edition of Energy Trends statistical publication.
